intent: process the first sample with scanpy

blocks.text: I'll present a plan for processing the first sample with scanpy before executing.

blocks.content: {"status": "ok", "name": "scrna-qc-clustering", "body": "scRNA-seq QC+clustering (scanpy): read 10x; calculate_qc_metrics; filter n_genes>=200 & pct_mito<0.20; normalize_total(1e4)+log1p; highly_variable_genes; pca; neighbors; umap; leiden; rank_genes_groups. Plots: HVG plot and PCA variance side-by-side; UMAP colored by leiden; use sc.pl.* not hand-rolled matplotlib."}

blocks.text: Now let me present the plan:

input.title: Standard scanpy QC and clustering for Patient 145 Day 0
input.summary: Load the first sample (GSM5746259), perform quality control, normalization, dimensionality reduction, and unsupervised clustering using scanpy.

steps.title: Load count matrix
steps.description: Read the 10x MTX files for GSM5746259 (Patient 145, Day 0) using scanpy

steps.title: Calculate QC metrics
steps.description: Compute n_genes, n_counts, and percent mitochondrial reads per cell

steps.title: Filter cells and genes
steps.description: Remove cells with <200 genes or >20% mitochondrial reads; remove genes with <3 cells
expected_outputs: QC plot showing thresholds; filtered object dimensions

steps.title: PCA
steps.description: Principal component analysis on HVGs; compute 50 PCs

steps.title: Neighborhood graph and UMAP
steps.description: Compute neighborhood graph (k=15) and UMAP embedding

steps.title: Cluster visualization
steps.description: UMAP colored by leiden cluster with cluster markers heatmap

assumptions: Using Patient 145 Day 0 sample (GSM5746259) only; will not combine with Day 7
Standard scanpy defaults: mitochondrial content <20%, min 200 genes/cell, ~2000 HVGs, Leiden resolution=1.0
Gene nomenclature: symbols are in column 1 of features.tsv (already loaded)
Human mitochondrial genes identified by 'MT-' prefix
Output will be a processed .h5ad object + standard plots (QC, HVG, PCA, UMAP, cluster)
